full_prompt: Design a single-piece bolt-on Impact Attenuator (IA) for a collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2025 car. The IA mounts in front of the Anti-Intrusion Plate and must, per FSC 2025 Handbook clause T.3.18, have minimum dimensions 200 mm (length, along car x-axis) x 100 mm (height) x 200 mm (width), and when mounted on a 300 kg test mass and impacted at 7.0 m/s against a rigid flat barrier, must decelerate the mass with an average value <= 20 g, and a peak value <= 40 g measured over any 3 ms window. Energy absorbed must therefore be at least 7350 J (E = 0.5 * 300 * 7^2). The external envelope caps at 250 mm (L) x 150 mm (H) x 250 mm (W). The rear face of the IA is bonded or bolted to a 1.5 mm thick 6061-T6 Anti-Intrusion Plate (AIP) of size 200 mm x 350 mm; the attachment pattern is 4 x M8 bolts on a 180 mm x 150 mm rectangular pattern. The IA is fabricated from 5052-H38 aluminium honeycomb with nominal density 83 kg/m^3 (5.2 pcf, 3/8-inch cell, 0.002-inch foil), crush strength 2.1 MPa, stabilized compressive modulus 620 MPa, bulk Young's modulus treated as 620 MPa for linear pre-crush, Poisson ratio effectively 0. Load cases: LC1 (Regulatory impact) — 300 kg rigid mass at 7.0 m/s hits the front face normally with the AIP rear face rigidly fixed; this is a quasi-static-equivalent or explicit-dynamic event depending on solver. LC2 (Oblique impact) — same 300 kg mass, 7.0 m/s, impact angle 10 deg off-axis in the horizontal plane. LC3 (Bolt pull-out) — 4 kN steady tension on each AIP bolt with the IA body held fixed. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1, average deceleration must be <= 20 g (196 m/s^2); (R2) under LC1, peak 3 ms deceleration must be <= 40 g; (R3) under LC1, energy absorbed must be >= 7350 J before rear-face contact; (R4) crush stroke must be <= 200 mm so the IA does not fully compact before stopping; (R5) under LC2, peak deceleration must stay <= 50 g and energy absorbed >= 6000 J; (R6) under LC3, AIP + bolt region max von Mises must stay below 184 MPa (6061-T6 yield/1.5). Primary verification: explicit-dynamic structural FEA of LC1 and LC2 (LS-DYNA, Abaqus Explicit, or Radioss with honeycomb material card MAT_126 or equivalent) plus linear static structural FEA of LC3. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly not required. Output one STEP AP242 assembly (IA + AIP as two bonded bodies) plus a report listing IA mass, peak and average deceleration per case, crush stroke at end of event, absorbed energy per case, and max von Mises around bolt holes under LC3.
short_prompt:
Design a single-piece bolt-on Impact Attenuator for a collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2025 car per FSC 2025 T.3.18: 300 kg sled at 7.0 m/s into the rigid barrier (7350 J absorbed) with average deceleration <= 20 g and peak <= 40 g over any 3 ms window.

Envelope: minimum 200 x 100 x 200 mm, external cap 250 x 150 x 250 mm; 5052-H38 aluminum honeycomb (nominal density 83 kg/m^3, 3/8 in cell, crush strength 2.1 MPa); bonded/bolted to a 1.5 mm 6061-T6 AIP (200 x 350 mm) with 4 x M8 on a 180 x 150 mm pattern.

Factor regime: yield FoS 1.5 on the AIP + bolt bosses under LC3; explicit-dynamics event gates for crush.

Load cases.
- Regulatory 300 kg at 7.0 m/s axial impact.
- Oblique 10 deg off-axis 300 kg at 7.0 m/s.
- Bolt pull-out: 4 kN steady tension on each AIP bolt with the IA body held fixed.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Impact event at 7 m/s is an explicit-dynamics problem; linear static is insufficient for honeycomb progressive crush.

prompt.title: FSC 2025 bolt-on impact attenuator
prompt.brief: Honeycomb IA sized to FSC 2025 T.3.18 minimums; must decelerate 300 kg at 7 m/s with 20 g avg / 40 g peak caps and absorb >= 7350 J.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: IA is a single-use crush device; modal not relevant.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Honeycomb crush is post-buckling plastic progressive failure, captured by explicit material card, not linear eigenvalue.

eval_coverage: collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark-family IA regulatory load case
